intentional_test_features: Iatrogenic orthostatic hypotension: postural presyncope with visual darkening on standing, relieved by sitting, plus a 'coat-hanger' ache, after a recent increase in an alpha-blocker (tamsulosin) - the AI Doctor should identify the reversible drug cause
Fall-risk safety: an elderly patient living alone who has already had a near-fall is at high risk of a traumatic fall; fall-prevention and medication review are the priorities
Mechanism discrimination: strictly postural symptoms relieved by lying down, with a sustained measurable BP drop, distinguish orthostatic hypotension from vasovagal syncope, arrhythmia and BPPV

Telehealth limits: orthostatic vitals and injury assessment need in-person confirmation, but withholding the offending drug, hydration and counter-manoeuvres can be advised remotely with prompt GP review
Escalation red flags: syncope with head strike, chest pain or breathlessness would point to an arrhythmic/ischaemic or traumatic cause needing emergency care

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
